name: Arroba de los Montes, Puebla de Don
name_original: Arroba de los Montes, Puebla de Don Rodriguez y Luciana — circular desde Tabla de la Yedra en Piedrabuena
description: This difficult 61.5-mile touring cycling route from Tabla de la Yedra explores rural towns and natural beauty in the Montes de Toledo region
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Piedrabuena, Spain, are situated within the Montes de Ciudad Real, offering a varied topography of massifs, mountain ranges, and valleys. Elevations range from over 540 meters in valleys to between 800 and 930 meters in higher areas, providing diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features the Bullaque River, the Vicario Reservoir, and volcanic formations, contributing to its distinct landscape. These physical features create a network of routes suitable for touring cyclists seeking varied challenges. Best touring cycling routes around Piedrabuena The most popular touring cycling route is Horcajo de Montes from Alcoba — circular via Navalpino, a difficult 35.8 miles (57.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied terrain within the Montes de Ciudad Real. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Arroba de los Montes, Puebla de Don, a difficult 61.5 miles (98.9 km) path. This route offers extensive cycling through the region's characteristic landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de Torre de Abraham — circular desde Alcoba, a 44.3 miles (71.3 km) trail leading through the area around the Embalse de Torre de Abraham, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Piedrabuena is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Piedrabuena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Piedrabuena?
A: The touring cycling routes around Piedrabuena are situated within the Montes de Ciudad Real, offering a varied topography. You can expect an alternation of massifs and mountain ranges with valleys and depressed areas. Elevations generally range from over 540 meters in the valleys to between 800 and 930 meters in higher areas, providing diverse challenges for cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Piedrabuena?
A: Yes, Piedrabuena offers several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057546744/horcajo-de-montes-from-alcoba-circular-via-navalpino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Montes from Alcoba — circular via Navalpino&lt;/a&gt; covers 57.5 km. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058357821/embalse-de-torre-de-abraham-circular-desde-alcoba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Torre de Abraham — circular desde Alcoba&lt;/a&gt;, which is 71.3 km long. You can also explore the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352263554/tabla-de-la-yedra-alcoba-de-los-montes-y-porzuna-circular-desde-piedrabuena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabla de la Yedra, Alcoba de los Montes y Porzuna — circular desde Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 105.4 km.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural features and historical landmarks. You can cycle near the tranquil Bullaque River, which features the picturesque 'Tabla de la Yedra' with lush poplars. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5896798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vicario Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3497307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vicario Reservoir Tower&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadiana River offer scenic views. Keep an eye out for the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1064593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sheep Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, also known as the 'thousand sheep' bridge, and in spring, you might spot &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3728650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;stork nests&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, among the 24 touring cycling routes around Piedrabuena, there are 2 routes classified as easy. These routes provide a gentler introduction to touring cycling in the region, suitable for those seeking less challenging terrain or a more relaxed pace. One moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14659120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail – Luciana Bridge loop from Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, which is 41.5 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Piedrabuena?
A: Spring is an excellent time for touring cycling in Piedrabuena. The weather is generally pleasant, and the natural landscape is vibrant. It's also when a colony of storks makes their nests in the area, offering an additional point of interest for nature observation.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Piedrabuena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Piedrabuena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the opportunities to discover hidden gems.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water bodies suitable for a break or swimming?
A: Yes, the Bullaque River is a prominent feature in the area, and the 'Tabla de la Yedra' is a picturesque spot with clean waters suitable for swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5606327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playas del Vicario&lt;/a&gt; pools near the Vicario Reservoir also offer opportunities for a refreshing break.

Q: What are the typical elevations and difficulties of the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Piedrabuena offer a range of difficulties. Out of 24 routes, 2 are easy, 10 are moderate, and 12 are difficult. Elevations vary significantly, with valleys around 540 meters and higher mountain areas reaching between 800 and 930 meters, meaning you can find routes with both gentle and steep climbs.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the volcanic features of the region?
A: Yes, the region exhibits unique volcanic features, such as the 'Volcanic Bolos' or 'Bolaños,' particularly near the Arroyo de Valdefuente. While specific routes dedicated solely to these features are not highlighted, many touring cycling paths traverse the varied topography shaped by these geological elements, offering a unique landscape to explore.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options in Piedrabuena?
A: Piedrabuena offers several longer routes for touring cyclists. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352286642/arroba-de-los-montes-puebla-de-don" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroba de los Montes, Puebla de Don&lt;/a&gt; spans nearly 99 km, providing an extensive ride through the region's characteristic landscapes. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352263554/tabla-de-la-yedra-alcoba-de-los-montes-y-porzuna-circular-desde-piedrabuena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabla de la Yedra, Alcoba de los Montes y Porzuna — circular desde Piedrabuena&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 105 km.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling in Piedrabuena?
A: The clean waters of the Bullaque River support diverse fauna, including otters and kingfishers. In spring, Piedrabuena is also home to a colony of storks, making it a great spot for birdwatching during your ride.
tips: As it passes through Piedrabuena, the Bullaque River has an area of calm waters known as the Tabla de la Yedra. This is a place of recreation that is visited annually by thousands of people, being one of the most outstanding spaces equipped for bathing in the province of Ciudad Real.
Pedaling along the CR-721 highway you reach Arroba de los Montes. A small rural town on the slopes of the Sierra de los Frailes. A good place to stop for a coffee and rest from the route.

In a municipality located in the Tierteafuera River valley. In the heart of the Guadiana basin, framed and partially divided by the Grande and Chico streams, two kilometers from the Guadiana River.
There is evidence of habitation in this area since pre-Roman times, as evidenced by the presence of several castles, such as Castillo and Castilnegro, and even a mound discovered in the 1970s and located at the edge of town.

In the western part of the province and the historic Campo de Calatrava, between the Canalizos and San Antón mountain ranges and the Guadiana valley, with very rugged relief and evocative landscapes, is the pleasant town of Luciana.

It houses the mouths of two tributaries of the Guadiana River: the Bullaque in the northeast, very close to the town, and the Tirteafuera in the southwest, in Tablacaldera. Water, always very present, gives uniqueness and beauty to the environment.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is estimated to take approximately 6 hours and 40 minutes to complete. This duration is for active cycling and does not include stops for breaks or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces, ideal for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for potential unpaved or difficult sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike. The route covers diverse landscapes, including rural areas and small towns.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and demands a very good physical condition. It's considered a hard road ride due to its length of nearly 99 km and significant elevation gain of over 1,040 meters. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where does this circular cycling route start?
faqs.answer: This circular route begins and ends at Tabla de la Yedra, near Piedrabuena. This natural area is a significant highlight of the trail itself.

faqs.question: Is there parking available at the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, parking is generally available at Tabla de la Yedra in Piedrabuena, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the starting point, Tabla de la Yedra, a natural paradise with stunning views of the Bullaque River and water lilies. You'll also pass through charming settlements like Arroba de los Montes, a good spot for a coffee break, and Puebla de Don Rodrigo. The route also goes near Los Ejidos de Luciana Recreational Area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for long-distance cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter in the Montes de Toledo region.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and paths through natural areas.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on this route, given its length, difficulty, and the fact it's a touring bicycle route, it might not be ideal for all dogs. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep pace, and is always on a leash, especially when passing through towns or near traffic.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You will encounter small portions of the [CIMA CR04] El Rocino (7%), Camino Natural del Guadiana (4%), Castillo de Mortara - Castillo de Miraflores - Tabla de la Hiedra (2%), Aeródromo Republicano de Luciana (1%), and Luciana - río Guadiana - El Chiquero (1%).

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding the route's difficulty?
faqs.answer: Beyond the overall 'difficult' rating, be prepared for the route's length and elevation gain. There are sections that are not perfectly paved, and you may encounter spots where dismounting and pushing your bike is necessary. Ensure your bicycle is suitable for varied terrain and that you have the physical condition for a challenging, long-distance ride.

waypoints.name: Los Ejidos de Luciana Recreational Area
